gloss.en: person who leaves school before finishing

words.example: He was a high school dropout.

gloss.en: long period with little or no rain

words.example: Farmers are facing ruin after two years of severe drought.

gloss.en: a fight between two people

words.example: The two men fought a duel at dawn.

gloss.en: not interesting or exciting

words.example: Life in a small town could be deadly dull.

gloss.en: get rid of unwanted things carelessly

words.example: Too much toxic waste is being dumped at sea.

gloss.en: an exact copy of something

words.example: Please make a duplicate of this key.

gloss.en: act of copying or repeating something

words.example: We must avoid the duplication of work.

gloss.en: length of time something lasts

words.example: The school was used as a hospital for the duration of the war.

gloss.en: a very small person or creature

words.example: In the story, seven dwarfs lived in the forest.

gloss.en: substance used to color cloth or hair

words.example: She used a red dye for the cloth.

gloss.en: in a keen, excited way

words.example: The children waited eagerly for the show.

gloss.en: a large strong bird that hunts

words.example: An eagle flew high above the mountains.

gloss.en: towards the east

words.example: The ship sailed eastward across the ocean.

gloss.en: towards the east

words.example: They traveled eastwards for many days.

gloss.en: in a way relating to the environment

words.example: This product is ecologically friendly.

gloss.en: scientist who studies living things and environment

words.example: The ecologist studied the forest animals.

gloss.en: using money or resources without waste

words.example: This car is very economical to run.

gloss.en: study of money, trade and industry

words.example: He studied politics and economics at Yale.

gloss.en: person who studies or writes about economics

words.example: The economist predicted slow growth this year.

gloss.en: very happy and excited

words.example: She was ecstatic when she won the prize.

gloss.en: correct and improve text for publishing

words.example: This draft text will need to be edited.

gloss.en: form in which a book is published

words.example: I bought the paperback edition of the book.

gloss.en: connected with preparing things to publish

words.example: She joined the magazine's editorial staff.

gloss.en: having had good schooling or training

words.example: He is a well educated young man.

gloss.en: in a way that produces a good result

words.example: The company must reduce costs to compete effectively.

gloss.en: working well without wasting time or energy

words.example: She is a highly efficient worker.

gloss.en: in a way without wasting time or energy

words.example: The event was very efficiently organized.

gloss.en: the joint in the middle of the arm

words.example: She jabbed him with her elbow.

gloss.en: a polite word for old

words.example: An elderly man sat on the bench.

gloss.en: choose someone by voting

words.example: Voters will elect a new president next year.

gloss.en: chosen by you; not required

words.example: Art is an elective subject at the school.

gloss.en: person whose job is fixing electrical things

words.example: We called an electrician to fix the lights.

gloss.en: using electronic equipment or computers

words.example: You can pay for the goods electronically.

gloss.en: science and technology of electronic equipment

words.example: He works in the electronics industry.

gloss.en: quality of being graceful and stylish

words.example: The room was decorated with great elegance.

gloss.en: attractive and showing good style

words.example: She was tall and elegant.

gloss.en: in an attractive and stylish way

words.example: She was elegantly dressed for the party.

gloss.en: basic and powerful; like natural forces

words.example: The storm had an elemental force.

gloss.en: connected with the first stages of study

words.example: She teaches an elementary English course.

gloss.en: remove or get rid of something

words.example: Credit cards eliminate the need to carry cash.

gloss.en: skill of speaking well and persuasively

words.example: He spoke with great eloquence.

gloss.en: able to speak well and persuasively

words.example: She gave an eloquent speech to the crowd.

gloss.en: in or to another place

gloss.en: hold someone in your arms warmly

words.example: They embraced and promised to keep in touch.

gloss.en: come out and become possible to see

words.example: The crabs emerge at low tide to look for food.

gloss.en: sending out of gas, light or heat

words.example: We must reduce the emission of carbon dioxide.

gloss.en: connected with people's feelings

gloss.en: in a way connected with feelings

gloss.en: special importance given to something

gloss.en: give special importance to something

words.example: His speech emphasized the importance of education.

gloss.en: in a strong and clear way

words.example: She emphatically denied the rumor.

gloss.en: group of countries ruled by one government

words.example: These invasions led to the collapse of the Roman empire.

gloss.en: give someone a paid job

words.example: How many people does the company employ?

gloss.en: person who is paid to work

words.example: The firm has over 500 employees.

gloss.en: person or company that pays workers

words.example: They are very good employers.

gloss.en: make it possible for someone to do something

gloss.en: a meeting, often sudden or unexpected

gloss.en: a serious effort or attempt

gloss.en: in a way that never seems to stop

gloss.en: ability to keep going through hardship

words.example: The race tested the runners' endurance.

gloss.en: lasting for a long time

words.example: They built an enduring friendship.

gloss.en: strength and effort for activity

gloss.en: attract and hold someone's attention

words.example: It is a movie that engages both the mind and the eye.

gloss.en: surround and cover something completely

gloss.en: improve the quality or value of something

gloss.en: giving pleasure

words.example: We had an enjoyable weekend together.

gloss.en: official process to find out information

gloss.en: officially join a course or group

gloss.en: make sure something happens

words.example: We are working to ensure the safety of the city.

gloss.en: enjoyable to watch or listen to

words.example: He gave an entertaining speech.

gloss.en: strong excitement and interest in something

words.example: Her voice was full of enthusiasm.

gloss.en: showing a lot of excitement and interest

words.example: He is an enthusiastic supporter of the team.

gloss.en: with a lot of excitement and interest

words.example: The crowd cheered enthusiastically.

gloss.en: including all of something; whole

gloss.en: completely; in every way

words.example: The experience was entirely new to me.

gloss.en: person who starts businesses, often taking risks

words.example: The entrepreneur was always dreaming up new projects.

gloss.en: paper container for sending letters

words.example: She put the letter in an envelope.

gloss.en: the natural world around us

words.example: The government should do more to protect the environment.

gloss.en: in a way relating to the natural world

words.example: This product is environmentally friendly.

gloss.en: the same in size, value or amount

gloss.en: provide the things needed for something

gloss.en: fairness; or value of shares owned

gloss.en: equal in value, amount or meaning

gloss.en: a period of time in history

words.example: They lived in the modern era.

gloss.en: throw out burning rocks and smoke

words.example: The volcano could erupt at any time.

gloss.en: sudden throwing out of lava and smoke

words.example: The volcanic eruption destroyed the town.

gloss.en: person who writes essays

words.example: He was a famous essayist and poet.

gloss.en: when thinking about the basic nature

words.example: There are three essentially different ways to solve it.

gloss.en: start or create an organization or system

gloss.en: area of land with many similar buildings

words.example: She lives on an estate in London.

gloss.en: a rough judgement of size or cost

gloss.en: calculated roughly without exact figures

gloss.en: forever; for all time

gloss.en: moral principles guiding behaviour

gloss.en: connected with right and wrong

words.example: The doctor faced difficult ethical questions.

gloss.en: belonging to a cultural or racial group

words.example: Tell me about your ethnic background.

gloss.en: moving people from a dangerous place

words.example: The flood forced the evacuation of the town.

gloss.en: judge the value or quality carefully

words.example: They made an evaluation of the healthcare system.

gloss.en: able to be divided exactly by two

words.example: 4, 6, 8 and 10 are all even numbers.

gloss.en: lasting forever; never ending

words.example: They promised everlasting love.

gloss.en: clear and easily seen

gloss.en: in a way that is clear or obvious

gloss.en: morally bad and cruel

gloss.en: slow development of living things over time

gloss.en: develop gradually over time

gloss.en: making something seem bigger than it is

gloss.en: praise someone or something highly

gloss.en: person who takes a test or exam

gloss.en: be greater than a number or amount
